meaning: vi.呜咽,抽咽地哭,低声哭泣
examples: The main building in Waiba Temple consists of Pule Temple, Puning Temple, Xumifusi Temple.
The reason the cheese, known as pule, is so expensive is that it takes 25 litres of fresh donkey milk to make a single litre of cheese.
Those who managed to reach Pu 'uhonua were graced by Kahuna Pule following a complex redemption ritual after which they were free to return to their village and resume every day life.
Also back in the squad is the 'enfant terrible' of South African football, Jabu Pule, who was last in the national team set-up at the African Nations Cup finals in Tunisia in January.
